title: Tangy Uruguayan Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew featuring lamb, white wine vinegar, and ferment-kimchi, cooked to perfection in a tandoor-clay-pot. This dish is perfect for a cold and rainy day.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tandoor-clay-pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the white wine vinegar, ferment-kimchi, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1.5 hours.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Let the stew rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve with crusty bread or over rice.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for later use.
